geocode.source: Brooklyn: Atlantic Avenue - New York Avenue (East)

original_title: Brooklyn: Atlantic Avenue - New York Avenue (East)
text:
Atlantic Ave., east from a point east of the intersection of East New York and Atlantic Aves., showing at the left, the Central Hotel which later housed the Brooklyn Daily Eagle.  At the right background, is a turreted building, which was Jewell's 26th Ward Bank; established in 1888, by Ditmas Jewell at No. 2500 Atlantic Ave., in rented quarters, on March 25, 1889. The first location was at the corner of Georgia Ave.  The cornerstone for the new building was laid on July 7, 1891, and the building opened March 3, 1892.

1897.

Eugene L. Armbruster Collection.

geocode.source: Brooklyn: Atlantic Avenue - New York Avenue (East)

original_title: Brooklyn: Atlantic Avenue - New York Avenue (East)
text:
(1)
Atlantic Ave., east from the intersection of East New York and Atlantic Aves. The Long Island Railroad (LIRR) structure is seen on Atlantic Ave. The Brooklyn Trust Co. building in the right background now houses the Mechanics Bank.

April 1929.
Eugene L. Armbruster Collection

(2)
The same, a further view.
April 1929.
Eugene L. Armbruster Collection

geocode.source: Brooklyn: New York Avenue (East) - Atlantic Avenue

original_title: Brooklyn: New York Avenue (East) - Atlantic Avenue
text:
1. East New York Ave., from Atlantic Ave., to Broadway, showing in the background the Junction for all B.M.T. Elevated lines in this area.

May 1929.

Eugene L. Armbruster Collection.

2. East New York Ave. east of Williams Ave. Neg. A-206
P.L. Sperr, photographer
July 24, 1941

geocode.source: Brooklyn: New York Avenue (East) - Atlantic Avenue

original_title: Brooklyn: New York Avenue (East) - Atlantic Avenue
text:
East New York Ave., north side, between Atlantic Ave., and Pacific Street. In the background is the East New York Station of the Long Island Railroad.

July 27, 1930.

P. L. Sperr.
